title: STAT+: The HSA industry makes its MAHA pitch
summary: A new lobbying group tied to the health savings account industry is pushing hard to make HSAs far more widely available.

title: Lilly beefs up neuroscience pipeline with $6.3B Centessa buyout
summary: The deal secures two sleep disorder drugs that would fill a hole in the company’s portfolio and intensify a competition with Takeda and Eisai.

title: STAT+: HHS changes the U.S. health IT office’s name, and its purview
summary: The Trump administration is changing the name of the federal health IT office back to the Office of the National Coordinator.

title: STAT+: Baby KJ scientists hit speed bump in quest to scale custom gene editor
summary: The scientists who treated Baby KJ say the FDA's strict standards will make it much harder to create bespoke gene editing for others.

title: Viridian tumbles on positive data for key eye drug prospect
summary: The data did not meet investor expectations, and could bring “fierce investor debate on commercial feasibility,” according to an analyst.

title: Supreme Court rules against Colorado ban on ‘conversion therapy’ for LGBTQ kids
summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday ruled against a law banning “conversion therapy” for LGBTQ+ kids in Colorado, one of about two dozen states that ban the discredited practice.

title: STAT+: OpenEvidence inks deal with major health system
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: New OpenEvidence's partnership, pressure on HHS over health record sharing, and K health has a bot.

title: Biogen, with $5.6B Apellis buy, builds out immunology offerings
summary: Chief Financial Officer Robin Kramer explained how the deal not only provides two marketed products, but a team with nephrology expertise that will be valuable as Biogen prepares to launch…

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about pharma delaying launches in Europe, Lilly and Biogen deals, and more
summary: Drugmakers are delaying launches of some new medicines in Europe as the industry grapples with U.S. pressure ​and pricing policy shifts from President Trump

title: A new survey examines mental health outcomes of gender euphoria
summary: Lead exposure and heart disease, promoting psychedelics on YouTube, and other health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Psychedelic biotechs face scrutiny over YouTube promos
summary: Biogen and Eli Lilly make takeover moves, Doctors Without Borders slams Gilead, and more biotech news

title: STAT+: Whoop, maker of health and fitness tracking wristbands, valued at $10 billion after latest fundraising
summary: Whoop, the maker of health and fitness tracking wristbands, raised $575 million from investors including the Mayo Clinic and the sovereign wealth fund of Qatar.

title: Merck strikes deal with antibody discovery startup
summary: In an R&D collaboration worth as much as $838 million, Merck will lean on Infinimmune to discover immune cell-directed antibodies for multiple targets.

title: STAT+: Biogen to acquire immunology biotech Apellis for $5.6 billion
summary: Biogen is buying Apellis Pharmaceuticals for $5.6 billion upfront as it looks to expand its immunology business.

title: STAT+: Eli Lilly to buy Centessa Pharmaceuticals, maker of a narcolepsy drug, in $6.3B deal
summary: Eli Lilly is buying Centessa Pharmaceuticals in a deal worth at least $6.3 billion, picking up a promising narcolepsy treatment.

title: Biotech IPOs stayed at slow pace, but grew larger in the first quarter of 2026
summary: The six companies that have priced initial public offerings so far banked a median of $287.5 million, a figure far surpassing what’s been observed over a similar timeframe in prior years, according…

title: STAT+: Jimini Health raises funding for AI chatbot targeting complex mental health care
summary: Jimini Health has raised $17 million in seed funding as it seeks to launch its mental health AI platform Sage with large behavioral health organizations.

title: STAT+: Biotech VCs move upstream in China’s scientific pipelines as competition grows fiercer
summary: U.S. venture capital firms are no longer waiting for Chinese biotech assets to surface before investing in them — they’re moving upstream.

title: STAT+: Over-the-top psychedelic promos could undermine the field’s drug development efforts
summary: How over-the-top social media videos about psychedelics could undermine the field’s efforts to get its drugs approved.

title: STAT+: HSA companies struck it big in Trump’s tax bill. They’re lobbying for more
summary: The HSA industry is echoing MAHA as it pushes to expand the market for tax-free savings accounts.

title: Opinion: How the next CDC director can win back America’s trust
summary: “The CDC stands at an inflection point,” write Charles J. Lockwood, Robert C. Gallo, and Sten H. Vermund.

title: Opinion: America needs more clinics of last resort for patients who can’t get answers
summary: “They never gave us false hope, but they promised to try everything”: an excerpt from “The Elusive Body” by Alexandra Sifferlin.

title: STAT+: Pharma giant Takeda to lay off nearly 250 workers in latest job cutting
summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals, the largest biopharma employer in Mass., will lay off 250 workers in Cambridge beginning in July.

title: Blackstone closes $6.3B fund for life sciences investing
summary: It's the biggest life sciences private fund ever raised, coming in nearly 40% larger than its Blackstone predecessor vehicle.

title: STAT+: Doctors Without Borders calls Gilead ‘unconscionable’ for refusing to sell HIV prevention drug to the organization
summary: The move came after months of talks in which Doctors Without Borders asked Gilead Sciences for a “limited” supply of lenacapavir.

title: STAT+: Why this Stanford psychiatrist thinks diet can influence serious mental health disorders
summary: Shebani Sethi is at the vanguard of metabolic psychiatry, a field that investigates the link between diet and serious mental health disorders.

title: United rises on IPF results; Biogen scores positive lupus data and an approval
summary: Tyvaso, a marketed PAH therapy from United Therapeutics, continued to show potential in another lung condition. Elsewhere, Aurinia inked an acquisition and Lilly built its case for Ebglyss.

title: Lilly’s AI commitment expands through deal with Insilico
summary: A collaboration “across multiple therapeutic areas” could net the Hong Kong-based biotech more than $2 billion as Lilly seeks to sustain its industry lead.

title: Obesity drugmaker Kailera plans an IPO
summary: The company, led by Ron Renaud, raised $1 billion in venture funding to develop a competitor to Wegovy and Zepbound that’s now in late-stage testing.

title: Sanofi eczema drug weighed down by mixed results, safety concerns
summary: Despite hitting key goals across three late-stage studies, the drug, which targets the “OX40” ligand, raises safety and efficacy questions that had at least one analyst lower his sales forecast.

title: ROCK2 is a druggable angiocrine target in liver fibrosis
A renaissance in targeting the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway
First-in-class blood–brain barrier shuttle wins FDA nod for Hunter syndrome
FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Kresladi (marnetegragene autotemcel), the first gene therapy for the treatment of severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency Type I (LAD-I).

title: Oral macrocyclic IL-23 inhibitor nabs FDA approval for psoriasis
FDA Approves Drug to Treat Neurologic Manifestations of Hunter Syndrome
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Avlayah (tividenofusp alfa-eknm) to treat certain individuals with Hunter syndrome (Mucopolysaccharidosis type II or MPS II).
